description:
You are a maritime training instructor at Warsash Maritime School preparing a COLREGS (International Regulations for Preventing Collisions at Sea) night assessment exercise for cadets. Using Bridge Command, create a nighttime multi-vessel scenario in the Solent that tests head-on (Rule 14), crossing (Rule 15), and overtaking (Rule 13) encounter types. Save the scenario as a directory named 'n) Solent COLREGS Night Assessment' in /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/. 

The scenario must include an environment.ini file with nighttime conditions (StartTime between 22.0-4.0, Setting='Solent', VisibilityRange >= 5.0, Weather <= 2.0) and an ownship.ini file placing the vessel 'MV Dorado' at Solent coordinates (Lat ~50.77-50.82, Long ~ -1.20 to -1.10) at 10 knots on a heading of approximately 090. The othership.ini must contain exactly four vessels, each with at least two waypoint legs: a head-on container ship (reciprocal course ~270, 12-15 kts), a crossing-from-starboard bulk carrier (course ~000 or ~180, 8-12 kts), an overtaking fast ferry (same general direction as own ship, 15-20 kts, starting from behind), and a fishing vessel displaying restricted manoeuvrability. Configure bc5.ini for ARPA radar with arpa_on=1, full_radar=1, radar_range_resolution=256, and max_radar_range=96. Finally, write an assessment briefing document to /home/ga/Documents/colregs_assessment_briefing.txt listing all four encounter types, the expected cadet responses under COLREGS rules, and vessel identifiers. Login: ga / password123.
